skill.name: convex-migration-helper
skill.description: Plans and executes safe Convex schema and data migrations using the widen-migrate-narrow workflow and the @convex-dev/migrations component. Use this skill when a deployment fails schema validation, existing documents need backfilling, fields need adding or removing or changing type, tables need splitting or merging, or a zero-downtime migration strategy is needed. Also use when the user mentions breaking schema changes, multi-deploy rollouts, or data transformations on existing Convex tables.
skill.summary: Plans and executes safe Convex schema and data migrations using the widen-migrate-narrow workflow.
skill.author: get-convex

security_audit.summary: All 97 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill consists of documentation and TypeScript code examples for Convex database migrations. Pattern 'v.optional' flagged as weak crypto is actually a Convex schema validator. Backtick patterns are markdown code formatting, not shell execution. URLs are documentation links, not network calls. No actual code execution, credential access, or network operations exist in this documentation-only skill.

content.user_title: Plan and execute safe Convex database migrations
content.value_statement: Breaking schema changes in Convex can fail deployment or corrupt data. This skill guides you through the widen-migrate-narrow workflow for zero-downtime migrations with backfilling and data transformation.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes breaking schema changes and plans multi-deploy migration workflows
Generates TypeScript migration code using @convex-dev/migrations component
Implements widen-migrate-narrow pattern for safe schema evolution
Creates batched, resumable migrations with dry-run support
Provides zero-downtime strategies including dual-write and dual-read patterns
Generates verification queries to confirm migration completion
limitations: Cannot directly execute Convex CLI commands - provides commands for user to run
Requires access to your Convex project files and schema definitions
Cannot monitor live migration progress without user-provided status output
Large table migrations require manual execution and monitoring by user

use_cases.title: Add required field to existing table
use_cases.description: Widen schema to allow optional field, backfill existing documents, then narrow schema to require the field.
use_cases.target_user: Convex developers adding new required fields to production tables

use_cases.title: Split nested data into relational structure
use_cases.description: Extract nested objects into separate tables with proper foreign key relationships while preserving data integrity.
use_cases.target_user: Teams normalizing their Convex data model for better query performance

use_cases.title: Change field type with data transformation
use_cases.description: Create new field with target type, migrate existing data with transformation, then deprecate old field.
use_cases.target_user: Developers refactoring data types without production downtime

prompt_templates.title: Beginner - Add optional field
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to add a new optional field 'bio' to my users table in Convex. Show me the schema change and explain if I need a migration.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple schema addition that does not require migration

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate - Add required field with backfill
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to add a required 'role' field to my users table that already has data. Help me plan the widen-migrate-narrow workflow and generate the migration code using @convex-dev/migrations.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced - Split nested data into table
prompt_templates.prompt: My users table has a nested 'preferences' object I want to split into a separate userPreferences table. Plan the migration including the dual-write strategy and generate all necessary code.

prompt_templates.title: Expert - Full migration workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my schema changes in convex/schema.ts and identify all breaking changes. Create a complete migration plan with deploy commands, migration definitions, dry-run instructions, and verification queries for each step.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive migration audit and execution plan

output_examples.input: I need to change the 'isPro' boolean field to a 'plan' enum with values basic, pro, and enterprise.
output: Deploy 1: Add optional 'plan' field alongside existing 'isPro'
Migration: Convert isPro to plan (true→pro, false→basic), then remove isPro
Deploy 2: Make 'plan' required and remove 'isPro' from schema
Code includes dual-write logic to maintain both fields during migration window

output_examples.input: How do I verify my migration completed successfully?
output: Query to check unmigrated documents with filter on undefined target field
Use migrations component getStatus for progress monitoring
Sample with limit of 10 document IDs for manual inspection if incomplete

best_practices: Always test migrations with dryRun:true before running on production data
Write the new format before migrating so documents created during migration are not missed
Deprecate fields with v.optional and comments instead of deleting when uncertain
anti_patterns: Making a field required before migrating existing data - deployment will fail
Using .collect() on large tables - use migrations component for batched pagination
Deleting migration code before confirming stability - keep until certain no rollback needed

faq.question: Why can't I just deploy the new schema directly?
faq.answer: Convex validates existing data against the schema. If documents have the old format, deployment fails. The widen-migrate-narrow workflow ensures data compatibility at each step.

faq.question: Do I always need the @convex-dev/migrations component?
faq.answer: For small tables (few thousand documents), a single internalMutation works. For larger tables, the component provides batching, progress tracking, and automatic resume from failures.

faq.question: What happens if a migration fails halfway?
faq.answer: The migrations component tracks progress with cursors. When rerun, it resumes from where it left off. Completed documents are automatically skipped.

faq.question: Can I run multiple migrations at once?
faq.answer: Yes, define them in order and run with the multi-migration runner. If one fails, subsequent migrations stop. Rerun to retry from the failed point.

faq.question: How do I handle code that reads the data during migration?
faq.answer: Use dual-read strategy: check for new format first, fall back to old format. Or use dual-write: write both formats during migration window.

faq.question: When can I delete the old field and migration code?
faq.answer: After the final deploy completes and you verify all data uses the new format. Keep migration code briefly in case rollback is needed, then remove once stable.
